fastsurfer_artifact: Checkpoint URLs, axial/coronal/sagittal configs, Inference objects
freesurfer_match: Atlas and transform resource setup before volumetric labeling
deep_research_synthesis: Both pipelines resolve anatomical priors before subject-level segmentation, but FastSurfer loads learned CNN weights while FreeSurfer uses installed generative atlas resources.
medical_imaging_note: Both are structural T1 MRI segmentation pipelines that depend on stable adult neuroanatomical priors.
qa_validation_note: Validate checkpoint or atlas presence before batch processing because failures here invalidate every downstream row.
computer_vision_note: This is where the discriminative prior is instantiated for FastSurfer.
data_engineering_note: This is a dependency-resolution stage rather than an image-transformation stage.
data_science_note: The prior shifts from a hand-crafted generative atlas to supervised parameters learned from labeled datasets.
statistical_note: There is no subject-level uncertainty yet; failures here are deterministic setup errors.
fastsurfer_input_requirements: cfg YAML (yacs CfgNode); checkpoint files (.pth/.pt) or URL; LUT text/csv (FreeSurferColorLUT)
freesurfer_input_requirements: GCA atlas (.gca), LTA transform (.lta), m3d files; atlas directory ${GCADIR}
comparison_unit: Resource bundle (checkpoints, configs, LUTs, atlases, transforms)
secondary_metrics: Checksum or version parity; load latency; missing dependency count
space_harmonization: Compare normalized resource identifiers, hashes, and version strings; no image-space alignment required
expected_failure_modes: Missing checkpoint; stale atlas; corrupted download; LUT-version drift; device-incompatible weights
caveat: FreeSurfer does not have an automatic checkpoint-download analogue in standard recon-all.

fastsurfer_step: Conform input image and save conformed orig
fastsurfer_artifact: Conformed image at conf_name; optional copy to mri/orig/001.mgz
freesurfer_match: Import raw average and conform to canonical orig.mgz
deep_research_synthesis: This is the cleanest one-to-one mapping between the two pipelines.
medical_imaging_note: Conformation standardizes voxel geometry before anatomy is interpreted, which is critical for reproducible morphometry.
qa_validation_note: Check voxel size, orientation, data type, and field of view after conformation; regressions here propagate everywhere.
computer_vision_note: Both pipelines normalize spatial sampling before any anatomical classifier runs.
data_engineering_note: This is a schema-normalization step for image geometry and file placement.
data_science_note: It reduces nuisance variance from acquisition geometry and makes downstream models or atlases comparable.
statistical_note: It changes the support of the data but not the intended anatomical labels; interpolation effects are a known bias source.
fastsurfer_input_requirements: Input: T1-weighted MRI (mgz, nii, nii.gz) readable by nibabel; single-frame 3D; conform params: vox_size (float|'min'), orientation ('lia'|'native'), img_size bool; outputs a conformed nibabel image and numpy array
freesurfer_input_requirements: Input: rawavg.mgz (T1 average); output: orig.mgz conformed to FreeSurfer standards (typically 1mm isotropic, default 256^3 unless -cw256 used)
secondary_metrics: Affine delta; intensity correlation; header field parity; runtime
space_harmonization: Resample both outputs to a common 1 mm isotropic reference grid and compare voxel data separately from affine and header metadata
expected_failure_modes: Interpolation mismatch; crop or pad differences; orientation drift; intensity scaling discrepancy
caveat: FastSurfer can keep native orientation or field-of-view settings depending on flags, whereas recon-all defaults to a canonical conform.

fastsurfer_step: Reorient native data to soft LIA for inference
freesurfer_match: Implicit canonical orientation handling after recon-all conform
deep_research_synthesis: FastSurfer makes this axis handling explicit in code, while FreeSurfer largely hides it inside earlier conform/import conventions.
medical_imaging_note: Consistent orientation is essential when left-right neuroanatomical assignments are part of the label set.
qa_validation_note: Add left-right sanity checks on known asymmetric structures when modifying this area.
computer_vision_note: FastSurfer prepares a network-friendly tensor orientation instead of relying on later atlas transforms.
data_engineering_note: This is an internal representation change that should remain lossless for labels after inverse mapping.
data_science_note: It isolates coordinate-system nuisance factors from the classifier itself.
statistical_note: Orientation transforms are deterministic; the statistical risk is systematic hemisphere inversion rather than random noise.
fastsurfer_input_requirements: orig_data numpy ndarray 3D; affine 4x4 nibabel matrix; zoom tuple/list floats; result expected in soft-LIA orientation for network
freesurfer_function_calls: mri_convert/mri_vol2vol used by recon-all to set orientation (mri_convert --conform)
freesurfer_input_requirements: orig.mgz header used; mri_convert accepts nifti/mgz and outputs conform orientation; ensure header affine correct
secondary_metrics: Shape parity; reordered zoom parity; left-right landmark consistency
space_harmonization: Convert both outputs to canonical orientation descriptors and compare voxel-to-world transforms before any label metric
expected_failure_modes: Axis permutation bug; hemisphere flip; off-by-one transpose; stale zoom reorder
caveat: There is no distinct standalone FreeSurfer command corresponding only to this reorientation step.

fastsurfer_step: Run three-plane CNN inference with logit-space view aggregation
freesurfer_match: EM registration, canonical intensity normalization, non-linear atlas registration before labeling
deep_research_synthesis: This is the main substitution: FastSurfer collapses several atlas-driven FreeSurfer stages into one learned inference block.
medical_imaging_note: In medical-imaging terms, FastSurfer replaces iterative atlas fitting with a feed-forward anatomy estimator trained on brain MRI segmentation labels.
qa_validation_note: Benchmark accuracy separately on healthy brains, atrophy, lesions, motion, and anisotropic scans because failure modes differ from atlas methods.
computer_vision_note: The multi-view ensemble injects orthogonal-plane context and aggregates logits before hardening, which is a standard robustness pattern in volumetric CV.
data_engineering_note: This stage is the computational bottleneck and the main GPU/CPU scheduling concern.
data_science_note: Conceptually this replaces a generative posterior optimization with a discriminative approximation to the same anatomical labeling objective.
statistical_note: The output still lacks calibrated uncertainty; the later argmax yields a point estimate without confidence intervals.
fastsurfer_function_calls: For each plane: Inference.run(init_pred, image_name, orig_in_lia, _zoom_in_lia, out=pred_prob) invoked in RunModelOnData.get_prediction; Inference.setup_model->build_model->load_checkpoint
fastsurfer_input_requirements: orig_data: 3D numpy ndarray (values>0.8 max checked); orig_zoom: 3-element voxel sizes in mm; init_pred: torch tensor shape (X,Y,Z,num_classes) dtype float16 on viewagg_device; model cfg defines patch/pad sizes
freesurfer_function_calls: mri_em_register, mri_ca_normalize, mri_ca_register, mri_ca_label invoked by recon-all to register and label volumes (atlas-based)
freesurfer_input_requirements: Inputs: nu.mgz/brain.mgz, atlas (.gca), xform (.lta); outputs: norm.mgh, talairach.m3d, label volumes; input must be conform and brain-extracted for best results
secondary_metrics: Runtime; regional Dice after labeling; calibration surrogate on FastSurfer only; transform residuals on FreeSurfer side
space_harmonization: Raw logits are not directly comparable to atlas posteriors, so evaluate both after conversion to common subject-space labels on the same voxel grid
expected_failure_modes: Domain shift; poor contrast; anisotropy; atlas misregistration; GPU precision differences
caveat: run_prediction.py does not perform Talairach registration or explicit mri_ca_normalize-style intensity correction internally.

fastsurfer_step: Take argmax and map prediction back to native orientation
fastsurfer_artifact: Hard label tensor in native image space
freesurfer_match: Implicit hard labeling emitted by atlas-based FreeSurfer stages
deep_research_synthesis: FreeSurfer reaches the same kind of hard label volume, but without an explicit argmax-visible step in the driver script.
medical_imaging_note: For downstream neuroanatomical review, this is the first subject-space discrete segmentation that can be visually QC'd directly.
qa_validation_note: Validate that native-space labels still align with the conformed anatomy after inverse orientation restoration.
computer_vision_note: This is the hardening step from soft evidence to a single winning class per voxel.
data_engineering_note: It marks the transition from transient tensors to persistent segmentation artifacts.
data_science_note: This is a maximum-a-posteriori style decision rule applied after evidence aggregation.
statistical_note: Hardening discards probability mass information, so downstream QC needs separate heuristics to catch low-confidence failures.
fastsurfer_input_requirements: Input: pred_prob torch tensor float16 shape (X,Y,Z,num_classes); mapping labels tensor self.labels holds FS label IDs; split_cortex_labels expects numpy int array
freesurfer_function_calls: mri_ca_label and subsequent operations produce labels (no explicit argmax CLI; algorithm emits labels directly)
freesurfer_input_requirements: mri_ca_label inputs: normalized volumes, xform, gca atlas; may require mask and wm segmentation; outputs labeled vol in mgh/mgz format
secondary_metrics: 95th percentile Hausdorff distance; average symmetric surface distance; voxel accuracy; label-wise precision and recall
space_harmonization: Map both outputs to the same FreeSurfer label IDs and resample with nearest-neighbor interpolation into one subject grid before overlap metrics
expected_failure_modes: Orientation inversion; label collapse mismatch; jagged boundaries; tie-breaking instability
caveat: The nearest FreeSurfer analogue is distributed across several commands rather than represented as one explicit argmax operation.

fastsurfer_step: Map internal class indices to FreeSurfer label IDs
freesurfer_match: Label identity emission by FreeSurfer segmentation and cortical-volume mapping
freesurfer_artifact: aseg IDs plus aparc+aseg cortical IDs
deep_research_synthesis: The semantic target space is aligned to FreeSurfer so downstream tooling can consume the labels.
medical_imaging_note: This matters clinically and anatomically because label identity, not just shape, determines region-wise volumetric summaries.
qa_validation_note: Regression tests should compare label vocabularies and LUT consistency, not only voxel overlap.
computer_vision_note: FastSurfer separates model-class indexing from external ontology mapping, which is a safer CV design.
data_engineering_note: This is a schema-mapping step from model-native classes to an external interoperable standard.
data_science_note: It decouples representation learning from downstream analysis conventions.
statistical_note: Ontology mapping is deterministic; statistical error comes from upstream classification, not from the lookup itself.
fastsurfer_input_requirements: LUT file path (txt/csv) with columns ID, LabelName, R,G,B,A; labels array must include all label IDs used by model; expected integer IDs
freesurfer_function_calls: FreeSurfer constructs label IDs via mri_* tools and relies on FreeSurferColorLUT.txt
freesurfer_input_requirements: FreeSurferColorLUT.txt format: ID LabelName R G B A (located in $FREESURFER_HOME)
comparison_unit: Label ontology mapping plus mapped volume
secondary_metrics: Unmapped label count; duplicate ID count; per-label frequency delta; LUT checksum parity
space_harmonization: Normalize both outputs to one FreeSurferColorLUT version before any region-wise metric or confusion matrix
expected_failure_modes: Stale LUT; missing IDs; duplicate IDs; class-index drift
caveat: FreeSurfer produces the same label IDs through separate subcortical and cortical-volume stages rather than one explicit remap call.

fastsurfer_step: Split cortical labels into left and right DKT cortical labels
fastsurfer_artifact: Hemisphere-specific cortical parcel IDs in the full prediction volume
deep_research_synthesis: This row is where FastSurfer starts to resemble FreeSurfer's later surface-derived volumetric parcellation rather than its earlier aseg-only segmentation.
medical_imaging_note: Hemisphere-specific cortical labeling is necessary for anatomically meaningful lobar and regional cortical summaries.
qa_validation_note: Check for left-right parcel leakage and sulcal-bank speckling when validating cortical labels.
computer_vision_note: FastSurfer predicts cortical parcels volumetrically, whereas FreeSurfer derives them from surfaces and then projects them back to volume.
data_engineering_note: This is the first point where the artifact semantics move beyond subcortical aseg into full aparc+aseg territory.
data_science_note: It compresses a later downstream FreeSurfer transformation into the immediate segmentation path.
statistical_note: Cortical parcel volumes here are voxelized estimates and should not be treated as equivalent to surface-native cortical area or thickness measurements.
fastsurfer_function_calls: du.split_cortex_labels(pred_classes.cpu().numpy()); fuse_cortex_labels/clean_cortex_labels used for lateralization and cleanup
fastsurfer_input_requirements: Input: aparc array with cortical labels (>=2000), dtype int; relies on connected component heuristics for WM masks
freesurfer_function_calls: Surface processing + mri_aparc2aseg produce cortical labels in FreeSurfer (surface-driven), recon-all projects annot->aparc+aseg
freesurfer_input_requirements: Requires surface files (.white,.pial), annot files, and successful surface recon (recon-all)
secondary_metrics: Left-right parcel swap count; regional volume error; boundary F1
space_harmonization: Restrict comparison to cortical labels, normalize parcel IDs, and resample with nearest-neighbor interpolation into the same subject voxel grid
expected_failure_modes: Parcel leakage; hemisphere swap; sulcal speckle; surface-to-volume projection mismatch
caveat: This is only a partial analogue because FreeSurfer's cortical labels are generated after surface reconstruction, not inside its early volumetric stream.

fastsurfer_step: Save the full FastSurfer prediction volume
fastsurfer_artifact: subject.segfile, typically aparc.DKTatlas+aseg.deep.mgz when called from run_fastsurfer.sh
deep_research_synthesis: The closest FreeSurfer artifact is aparc+aseg.mgz, but FreeSurfer reaches it later and via surfaces.
medical_imaging_note: Both artifacts are used for neuroanatomical region-level summaries, but they are not produced by identical mechanisms.
qa_validation_note: File-level regression tests should compare headers, label ranges, and downstream tool compatibility, not just file existence.
computer_vision_note: FastSurfer emits a volumetric DKT-style parcellation directly from a CNN.
data_engineering_note: This is the main durable handoff artifact for downstream segmentation consumers.
data_science_note: It exposes the end-product used for many summary-statistics workflows.
statistical_note: Direct numeric comparison with FreeSurfer aparc+aseg should acknowledge different generative paths and possible systematic biases.
fastsurfer_input_requirements: pred_data numpy ndarray or torch tensor with integer label IDs; orig_img nibabelImage for header/affine; output path must be .mgz/.nii/.nii.gz
freesurfer_function_calls: mri_aparc2aseg writes aparc+aseg.mgz from surfaces and aseg
freesurfer_input_requirements: Requires surface outputs and aseg for projection; outputs mgh/mgz file with cortical+subcortical labels
secondary_metrics: Multiclass Dice; header compatibility; downstream tool pass rate; file size parity
space_harmonization: Compare after nearest-neighbor resampling to one subject grid and one label ontology, while validating headers separately from voxel content
expected_failure_modes: Header incompatibility; wrong dtype; missing labels; corpus-callosum treatment mismatch
caveat: FastSurfer's full prediction is generated before corpus callosum inpainting, which is handled later by a separate module in the wrapper script.

fastsurfer_step: Create brainmask from segmentation using dilation, erosion, and largest-component selection
fastsurfer_artifact: Binary brain mask derived from predicted labels
deep_research_synthesis: The artifact matches conceptually, but the causality is reversed: FreeSurfer builds the mask to help segmentation, FastSurfer builds the mask from segmentation.
medical_imaging_note: This is a brain-extraction step used to exclude non-brain tissue from morphometric summaries and later surface processing.
qa_validation_note: This row needs edge-case QA on orbital frontal regions, dura, neck remnants, and largest-component failures.
computer_vision_note: FastSurfer uses morphology on semantic labels instead of intensity-driven skull stripping such as watershed.
data_engineering_note: This is a derivative artifact with explicit morphological post-processing logic and connected-component filtering.
data_science_note: It converts dense anatomical labels into a coarser support mask for later modules and stats.
statistical_note: Morphological hyperparameters induce systematic boundary bias; they should be treated as fixed algorithmic choices, not random variation.
fastsurfer_input_requirements: Input: segmentation ndarray; dilation iterations dnum (int), erosion iterations enum (int); returns binary mask uint8 same shape as seg
freesurfer_function_calls: mri_watershed [opts] INVOL.mgz brainmask.auto.mgz; mri_mask T1.mgz mask.mgz $OUT
freesurfer_input_requirements: Input: T1/nu/orig volumes; preflood heights control watershed behavior; output binary mask mgz
secondary_metrics: 95th percentile Hausdorff distance; sensitivity; specificity; intracranial volume delta
space_harmonization: Binarize both masks and compare on the same conformed voxel grid, keeping morphology-derived versus intensity-derived provenance separate in analysis
expected_failure_modes: Neck inclusion; dura retention; frontal clipping; largest-component failure
caveat: The timing differs materially from FreeSurfer because brainmask.mgz is an early prerequisite there but a post-hoc derivative here.

fastsurfer_step: Reduce full aparc+aseg prediction to aseg.auto_noCCseg-style labels
fastsurfer_artifact: aseg without cortical parcel IDs and without CC segmentation
deep_research_synthesis: This is the clearest artifact-level match on the FreeSurfer side: both rows produce the pre-CC aseg volume.
medical_imaging_note: The reduced aseg is the conventional subcortical tissue map used by many downstream neuroimaging tools.
qa_validation_note: Validate that cortical parcel IDs are fully collapsed to 3 and 42 and that mask application does not erase valid deep structures.
computer_vision_note: FastSurfer first predicts the richer label space, then deterministically collapses it to aseg semantics.
data_engineering_note: This is a format-reduction step that broadens compatibility with older FreeSurfer downstream tools.
data_science_note: It preserves the subcortical decision while intentionally discarding finer cortical categorical detail.
statistical_note: The reduction itself is deterministic; disagreement with FreeSurfer here comes from the earlier full-label prediction, not the collapse rule.
fastsurfer_input_requirements: Input: full aparc+aseg ndarray; outputs aseg ndarray with cortical collapsed to 3/42
freesurfer_input_requirements: Input: aparc+aseg or surface-derived labels; outputs aseg in mgz format with integer labels (uint8)
secondary_metrics: Label-wise volume error; key-structure 95th percentile Hausdorff distance; confusion matrix
space_harmonization: Collapse both outputs to the same aseg label vocabulary and resample with nearest-neighbor interpolation to a single subject grid
expected_failure_modes: Incorrect cortical collapse; deep nuclei erosion; CC leakage; label-remap mismatch
caveat: FastSurfer defaults can save this artifact under a caller-provided path; run_fastsurfer.sh normally uses aseg.auto_noCCseg.mgz.

fastsurfer_step: Flip disconnected white-matter islands between hemispheres when morphology suggests misassignment
fastsurfer_artifact: Corrected aseg with fewer cross-hemisphere WM islands
freesurfer_match: Closest downstream consumer is corpus-callosum refinement rather than a direct equivalent cleanup stage
deep_research_synthesis: This is a FastSurfer-specific cleanup that prepares the aseg for later CC logic and reduces pathological search boxes.
medical_imaging_note: White-matter island errors are neuroanatomically implausible and can propagate into callosal or hemispheric summary measures.
qa_validation_note: Add adversarial tests with disconnected WM islands and severe atrophy because this heuristic can change hemisphere assignment.
computer_vision_note: The step is a morphology-plus-proximity correction heuristic layered on top of the CNN output.
data_engineering_note: It is a local topological repair stage, not a core segmentation stage.
data_science_note: It encodes a structural prior about hemispheric contiguity that is not learned explicitly at inference time.
statistical_note: This is a deterministic heuristic post-processing rule; its risk is bias under unusual anatomy rather than random instability.
fastsurfer_function_signatures: flip_wm_islands(aseg_data:np.ndarray)->np.ndarray; flips disconnected WM island labels based on proximity heuristics
fastsurfer_input_requirements: Input: aseg ndarray with WM labels (2,41); outputs corrected aseg ndarray
freesurfer_input_requirements: mri_cc reads subject's aseg and norm (optionally) and writes aseg including CC segmentation; inputs: aseg.mgz and norm.mgz if -norm used
secondary_metrics: Island count; disconnected-component volume; callosal boundary error
space_harmonization: Restrict evaluation to white-matter labels 2 and 41 and compare connected components in the same voxel space
expected_failure_modes: Overcorrection in atrophy; asymmetric pathology; small bridge artifacts; hemisphere flip
caveat: FreeSurfer has no single named cleanup step with the same behavior; mri_cc is only the nearest downstream stage affected by this issue.

fastsurfer_step: Save aseg and brainmask derivative files
freesurfer_match: Write brainmask and aseg outputs across FreeSurfer skull-strip and subcortical-label stages
deep_research_synthesis: The output names align closely with FreeSurfer conventions even though the generation order differs.
medical_imaging_note: These are the key volumetric derivatives consumed by later bias-field correction, stats, and surface steps.
qa_validation_note: Compatibility tests should open these files with FreeSurfer tools and verify dtypes, affine/header integrity, and expected label sets.
computer_vision_note: This row turns internal arrays into interoperable neuroimaging files.
data_engineering_note: It is the I/O boundary between computation and downstream pipeline integration.
data_science_note: Persisting intermediate derivatives enables modular reuse and error localization.
statistical_note: No new stochastic behavior is introduced at save time; this is where silent header corruption or dtype truncation would matter most.
fastsurfer_input_requirements: Data arrays pred_data/bm/aseg; orig_img header/affine for saving; dtype uint8 for aseg/brainmask; output filenames must use supported extensions (.mgz/.nii/.nii.gz)
freesurfer_function_calls: recon-all and internal mri_mask/mri_convert calls write brainmask.mgz and aseg files
freesurfer_input_requirements: Files must be mgh/mgz with correct headers and dtype (uint8 for labels); recon-all expects subject folder structure and standard filenames
secondary_metrics: Header parity; dtype correctness; file-open success; checksum stability
space_harmonization: Validate metadata first, then compare voxel content on a common grid only if both files are readable and label ontologies match
expected_failure_modes: Dtype truncation; affine corruption; compression failure; naming or path mismatch
caveat: FreeSurfer spreads the generation of these files across earlier and later commands instead of saving both from one code block.

fastsurfer_step: Run volume-based quick QC and emit warnings or QC log entries
fastsurfer_artifact: Subject-level QC flag based on total segmented volume
freesurfer_match: No direct standard recon-all analogue in the core volumetric driver
freesurfer_artifact: None in standard recon-all; downstream QA is usually external or manual
deep_research_synthesis: FastSurfer adds a lightweight guardrail that FreeSurfer's core driver largely leaves to external QA workflows.
medical_imaging_note: A gross total-volume check is a clinically relevant sanity screen because catastrophic failures often collapse brain volume unrealistically.
qa_validation_note: Treat this as a smoke test only; it should supplement, not replace, visual QA and regional plausibility checks.
computer_vision_note: This is post-hoc failure detection rather than segmentation itself.
data_engineering_note: It is a batch-friendly monitoring hook that can be logged and aggregated across cohorts.
data_science_note: It provides a simple scalar proxy for catastrophic failure without modeling regional nuance.
statistical_note: The threshold is a heuristic decision boundary, so false positives and false negatives are expected outside the training distribution.
fastsurfer_function_calls: check_volume(pred_data, seg_voxvol) -> bool; get_ventricle_bg_intersection_volume(...) for additional checks
fastsurfer_input_requirements: Input segmentation ndarray and voxel volume float (np.prod(header.get_zooms())); default threshold 0.70 L
freesurfer_function_calls: No direct recon-all QC; external QA tools (aseg.stats, qa tools) used
secondary_metrics: False-positive rate; AUROC; correlation with manual QC score
space_harmonization: Reduce both pipelines to subject-level QC labels or continuous scores; no image-space alignment required
expected_failure_modes: Threshold too coarse; cohort shift; false alarms in small brains; missed regional failures
caveat: The default threshold is a coarse liter-scale cutoff and does not quantify uncertainty or regional anatomical plausibility.
